current_capabilities: Verify a fixed P216-P222 evidence chain by SHA256 across 18 committed artifacts.
Trace deterministic lineage from bounded historical sample collection through quality review, feature construction, in-sample baseline evaluation, and time-split holdout evaluation.
Quantify current baseline behavior on the fixed sample: P219 baseline A accuracy 0.375, P219 baseline B accuracy 0.5, P221 baseline A accuracy 0.25, and P221 baseline B accuracy 0.3125.
Compare in-sample versus time-split degradation using the committed P220 and P222 dashboard evidence.
disclaimer: Historical evaluation evidence index only. Not live predictions, not betting advice.
historical_only_disclaimer: Historical evaluation evidence index only. Not live predictions, not betting advice.
limitations: Fixed bounded sample only: P216/P218/P219/P220 are based on 24 rows across 3 dates for the committed historical snapshot.
Feature engineering remains heuristic and source-bounded; the artifacts do not establish full point-in-time pregame context, complete count state, or a production feature contract.
P219/P220 evidence is in-sample on the same 24 rows used to derive the baseline rules, so it does not establish out-of-sample predictive ability.
P221/P222 holdout evidence is still small: 16 evaluated rows across 2 time-split holdout dates.
Baseline A and baseline B are deterministic reference heuristics, not trained production models.
P221 baseline B fallback coverage remains weak: 5 of 16 rows used global fallback, with fallback accuracy 0.0.
Current evidence does not establish live data readiness, point-in-time integrity, or production activation readiness.
This index reads committed artifacts only and does not independently refresh, extend, or relabel the historical sample.

next_step_options.decision_point: Authorize point-in-time feature and lineage contract design before any model design work.
next_step_options.why_now: The current evidence chain is historical and deterministic, but it does not prove point-in-time feature integrity.

next_step_options.decision_point: Expand the bounded historical sample and rerun the same artifact chain before interpreting baseline behavior more broadly.
next_step_options.why_now: The present evidence is limited to 24 in-sample rows and 16 holdout rows.

next_step_options.decision_point: Revisit target and feature design to reduce sparse classes and fallback-heavy baseline behavior before any model experimentation.
next_step_options.why_now: Baseline B still relies on global fallback for a material share of holdout rows and struggles on minority classes.

next_step_options.decision_point: Defer model design authorization until point-in-time integrity and broader historical coverage are explicitly demonstrated.
next_step_options.why_now: Nothing in the current evidence chain establishes live or production-readiness.
